captures.notes: Official Three.js docs links for WebGPURenderer and TSL attribution and implementation framing; local evidence below is generated from scratch for learning review.
Official Three.js TSL docs link used for node material framing; local evidence below is generated from scratch for learning review.
Local generated rebuild screenshot showing backend decision panels, TSL node-material blocks, fallback routes, and resource buffers; not copied from Three.js docs.
Local generated motion clip isolates fallback route state, backend readiness, node-material stability, and buffer ownership; not copied from Three.js docs.
Standalone local HTML/SVG exercise reconstructing the primitive with generated backend panels, node materials, fallback routes, buffer blocks, and CSS-only fallback motion.
Manifest lists only generated local learning evidence plus source-link attribution boundaries.

payload.learning_primitive: WebGPU renderer contract plus TSL node material
payload.visual_role: teaches how shader aesthetics depend on renderer capability and resource contracts

modules.review_notes: Complete for first-pass learning: still image, motion clip, and code note all show the same documentation primitive.

payload.fallback_model: backend panels and routes represent device support and WebGL2 fallback planning

modules.review_notes: Performance layer is complete as a source-bounded implementation planning model, not a benchmark.

payload.evidence_layer: local PNG proves backend/material/resource taxonomy, local GIF proves fallback motion, HTML proves the implementation-constraint rebuild recipe
payload.learning_primitive: renderer readiness map for review artifacts
payload.review_gap: official docs remain link-only; local rebuild teaches constraints without docs screenshots, examples, renderer source, or demos
payload.route_decision: use backend panels for environment readiness, fallback routes for implementation risk, node-material blocks for visual logic ownership, and buffer blocks for evidence-resource budgets
payload.source_trail: official Three.js WebGPURenderer and TSL docs plus generated local reconstruction
payload.uncertainty: exact renderer internals, future API stability, and device-specific behavior are not claimed

modules.review_notes: VULCA mapping is complete because source, evidence, review gap, route decision, and uncertainty are all represented.

summary: Official Three.js WebGPU/TSL documentation rebuilt as a local implementation-constraint primitive with backend panels, fallback routes, node-material blocks, buffers, motion, code, and asset evidence.
title: Three.js WebGPURenderer and TSL Documentation
why_relevant: Completes the atlas's implementation baseline for contemporary shader materials by making renderer capability and fallback constraints visible.
